name: Squid in Its Own Ink (Chipirones En Su Tinta)
recipeIngredient: 2 lb small fresh whole squid with ink sacs
recipeInstructions:
Sauce:
1. Finely chop 1 onion and 1 clove garlic.
2. Crush the bread crust into a fine texture.
3. Mix in 2 tablespoons fish broth reserved from cooking the squid.
4. Add ink from the sacs using a fine sieve or directly into the bowl, carefully puncturing it.
5. Blend all ingredients until smooth.
6. Incorporate 8 teaspoons tomato puree and simmer for a short time.
Squid Preparation:
1. Separate and clean the fresh squid, taking care not to break the ink sacs.
2. Set aside the ink sacs.
3. Cut tentacle pieces into one-inch chunks.
4. Fry onions and garlic in olive oil until they start to color.
5. Add tentacle pieces to the frying pan with oil and cook slowly while stirring.
6. Stir in chopped parsley and bread crumbs until well combined.
7. Stuff squid tails with the mixture, avoiding overfilling.
8. Seal the openings of the tails using a toothpick.
9. Fry the stuffed tails gently in olive oil until golden brown.
Combine:
1. Place sauce in a large skillet.
2. Add fried, stuffed squid and simmer for 15 minutes.
Makes three to four servings.
